title: Service Starts at 5pm Sharp
tagline: One kitchen, one critic possibly at table twelve, and a service that doesn't forgive mistakes.

wake_up.text: The market opens in an hour and the good fish sells fast. Today's tasting menu depends on what's actually good this morning, not what the printed menu says.

choices.text: Go to the market yourself, like always
Send your sous chef and get a head start on prep

mise_en_place.text: The walk-in is organized, the stocks are reducing, and for one quiet hour the kitchen is just you and the prep list. It won't last.

choices.text: Get to work

staff_issue.text: Your line cook on the fish station calls in sick — the same station handling tonight's signature dish. Service is in five and a half hours.

choices.text: Cover the station yourself tonight, on top of expo
Call in a favor from a chef friend down the street

inventory_problem.text: The delivery arrives short on the langoustines the tasting menu is built around. Twenty tables are booked tonight expecting that dish.

choices.text: Rework the dish around what actually arrived
86 the dish and simplify the menu for tonight

pre_service_briefing.text: The whole team lines up for the pre-service briefing. Reservations sheet shows twenty-two tables, one flagged "VIP" with no further detail.

choices.text: Brief the team and open the pass

service_start.text: The first tickets print. The pass gets loud in the good way — for now. Table twelve orders the tasting menu, all nine courses.

choices.text: Get the first course moving

critic_table.text: The server comes back from table twelve, plate in hand, barely touched. "They said the sauce is broken." A quiet word in the dining room confirms your worst guess: that's the food critic everyone's been whispering about.

choices.text: Remake the dish completely from scratch
Fix the sauce quickly and resend the same plate
Go out to the table yourself, apologize, and comp the course
Send the next course and let this one go

critic_outcome_remake.text: Twelve minutes, a completely new plate, sent out with a short apology on the ticket. It's the best version of the dish you've made all week.

choices.text: Get back to the rest of the pass

critic_outcome_adjust.text: The fixed sauce goes out fast. It's good, not great — but it's back on the table before the rest of the course falls behind.

choices.text: Get back to the rest of the pass

critic_outcome_personal.text: You wipe your hands, walk out of the kitchen, and own it face to face. Whatever they write tomorrow, tonight they saw the chef care.

choices.text: Get back to the rest of the pass

critic_outcome_ignore.text: The kitchen keeps moving. It's the pragmatic call on a night this busy — but the plate that went out is exactly what got sent back.

choices.text: Get back to the rest of the pass

equipment_failure.text: The reach-in cooler on the pastry station starts alarming mid-rush. Whatever's in there needs to move, now, or it's lost.

choices.text: Stop everything and move the contents yourself
Delegate it and stay focused on the pass

vip_walkin.text: Table twelve's server confirms it — a well-known food writer, and they've asked to meet the chef after the meal.

choices.text: Step out and meet them personally
Send your compliments and stay on the pass

dessert_course.text: Last course of the night going out. The kitchen's exhausted in the specific way that only a good, hard service produces.

choices.text: Send it

closing_kitchen.text: Last ticket's in. The pass goes quiet. Someone starts breaking down stations before you've even said the word.

choices.text: Supervise a full, proper breakdown and clean
Let the team handle it and start the books

staff_meal.text: The whole team eats together at the end of the night, the one meal nobody's plating for a customer.

choices.text: Sit down and eat with everyone
Skip it and finish tomorrow's ordering

post_service_review.text: Before anyone leaves, a quick word on how tonight went — what worked, what nearly didn't.

choices.text: Be honest about the rough moments, not just the wins
Keep it upbeat and send everyone home

end_shift.text: The lights go off on an empty dining room. Table twelve's review, whatever it says, posts sometime tomorrow.

choices.text: Lock up and go home

random_moment.text: Prep continues, and the kitchen fills with the small, unrepeatable moments that make up most of any given morning.

choices.text: The produce delivery arrives early and pristine
A minor knife slip — quickly bandaged, nothing serious
Someone puts on the kitchen's favorite playlist

triumphant.title: Service to Remember
triumphant.blurb: Every table left happy, the critic included. This is the night the kitchen tells stories about for years.

steady_hand.title: Clean Service
steady_hand.blurb: No disasters, no fireworks. Just a well-run kitchen doing exactly what it's supposed to.

ordinary_day.title: Just Tuesday
ordinary_day.blurb: A full house, a few hiccups, nothing that'll make the review either way. Tomorrow, the market again at five.

burned_out.title: Running on Empty
burned_out.blurb: You made it to close, but you're not sure you could plate another dish tonight if the building was on fire. It nearly was.

written_up.title: Rough Notice
written_up.blurb: Word is already getting around about tonight's service, and not the good kind. Table twelve's review is going to sting.
